title: carex longhiensis
intro: Carex longhiensis, also known as Long's sedge, is a type of perennial grass-like plant native to Japan and the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Carex longhiensis include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Carex longhiensis can be prepared in various forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
